result:
All changes are complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
AnkiDroid was missing the "Show play buttons on cards with audio" preference that exists in Anki Desktop (`HIDE_AUDIO_PLAY_BUTTONS` config key).

### Changes Made

**6 files modified:**

1. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/preferences.xml`** - Added preference key string:
   - `hide_audio_play_buttons_preference` = `"hideAudioPlayButtons"`

2. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/10-preferences.xml`** - Added user-facing strings:
   - `show_play_buttons_on_cards_with_audio` - Title
   - `show_play_buttons_on_cards_with_audio_summ` - Summary description

3. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/res/xml/preferences_appearance.xml`** - Added `SwitchPreferenceCompat` in the Reviewer category, placed after "Show button time"

4. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/preferences/AppearanceSettingsFragment.kt`** - Wired the preference to the collection config `ConfigKey.Bool.HIDE_AUDIO_PLAY_BUTTONS`. The UI shows "Show play buttons" (checked = show), but the backend stores "hide" (true = hidden), so the boolean is inverted.

5. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/HtmlGenerator.kt`** - Added `hidePlayButtons` parameter. When `hidePlayButtons` is true, `expandSounds()` strips AV references instead of adding play icons (matching Anki Desktop's behavior).

6. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt`** - Reads `ConfigKey.Bool.HIDE_AUDIO_PLAY_BUTTONS` from the collection config during initialization and passes it to the `HtmlGenerator`.

7. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/SoundKt.kt`** - Fixed the `stripAvRefs` function which had its arguments in the wrong order (`AV_REF_RE.replace("", text)` → `AV_REF_RE.replace(text, "")`).

### Design Decisions
- Uses `ConfigKey.Bool.HIDE_AUDIO_PLAY_BUTTONS` (protobuf enum value 13) which is the same config key used by Anki Desktop, ensuring the preference syncs between platforms
- When hiding play buttons, strips the `[anki:play:...]` references entirely (same as Anki Desktop's `strip_av_refs`)
- Audio playback still works via autoplay and keyboard shortcuts when buttons are hidden
